summary: ALL 9 LEVELS REQUIRE EXPANSION: 7 levels have only 1 archetype; 2 levels have 2; 0 levels meet the 3-archetype target. Question depth also thin in L1–L3 (3–4 per archetype). This is foundational Phase 1 data for Plan 13 expansion roadmap.

phase_1_critical: L1 (Halves): monolithic partition → needs identify + label + comparison variants
L2 (Halves Depth): needs third archetype to reach minimum; both existing archetypes below 5-q threshold
L3 (Thirds & Fourths): label archetype severely thin (2 q); needs 3+ more questions + third archetype
phase_2_bottleneck: L4–L5 (Make): currently solo make archetype per level; injecting snap_match and compare variants would deepen mastery before L6 transition
L6–L7 (Compare): single-archetype levels; adding benchmark + ordering variants could scaffold same-denom → same-numerator transition
phase_3_capstone: L8 (Benchmarks): thin on comparison and ordering prep
L9 (Order): only order questions; should include benchmark + mixed compare to solidify capstone skills

notes: Current curriculum is archetype-narrow (each level teaches one main interaction) but question-thin within each archetype (3–6 questions vs target 5+). Phase 1 of Plan 13 should: (1) expand L1–L3 question counts within existing archetypes, (2) add 1–2 new archetypes per level, (3) reorder archetypes pedagogically so prerequisites appear before dependents.
